content_en:
——— The Prophetic Medicine

In this case, the loved person might even feel dislike for the other party. Second, sometimes the loved person might be the cause for the love to be one-sided, such as a deficiency in his mannerism, shape, path, actions, appearance, and so forth. Third, due to a specific reason that prevents the loved person from sharing the same teelings with the other party.

When these reasons are not present, and the love is thus real, love will be shared by both parties.

Arrogance, envy, loving various positions and enmity by the disbelievers were the main reasons the Messengers were not more beloved by them than their own selves, families and offspring. When these barriers were removed from the hearts of the Messengers’ followers, their love for the Messengers was indeed more than the love they had for their own selves, wives, children and wealth.

Attainment of one's love object is its cure and other cures for love

The subject is that since love is one of the ailments in general, it does have a cure or several cures. If the lover has a legal path to take to attain his loved object, it will be his cure. In the Sahihain it is narrated that Ibn Mas'ud said that the Prophet b said:

"O young people! Whoever amongst you can afford marriage, let him marry. Those who cannot afford it should resort to fasting, for it will be a cure for them.”

The Prophet b has directed the people to the best methods to obtain what they love, a major and a minor method. He ordered the men to resort to the first choice, marriage, which is the perfect cure for this disease. Thus,

people should not prefer other solutions to the solution that the Prophet b recommended.

Tbn Majah narrated that the Prophet b said: "We never saw anything like marriage for lovers."

This is the meaning that Allah indicated when He

allowed in marriage both free and slave women when needed: Allah said:

"Allah wishes to lighten (the burden) for you; and man was created weak (cannot be patient to leave sexual intercourse with woman).” (4:28)

Therefore, Allah mentioned man's weakness in this Ayah and stated that He has made matters easy for him by allowing him to marry as many as he wishes, two, three or four. He In addition allowed him slave women in marriage if he wishes, so as to treat this ailment, passion, to cure the weakness in mankind and as ar: act of mercy

——— The Prophetic Medicine

[from Him] to His slaves.

When no legal method exists for the lover to attain his love object

If there is no legal method that the lover can attain his loved object or because of his inability to do so, or both, then passion will become a difficult disease in this case and the afflicted person needs to feel that he cannot satisfy it. It is a fact that when the heart feels hopelessness in something, it will no longer seek to acquire it,

If the disease, passion, still exists even under these hopeless conditions, then the nature of the person would have deviated and the person needs to try another solution, that is, attending to his state of mind. The person afflicted by passiun should convince himself that his heart's affection with what it cannot attain constitutes madness, and that this case is similar to whoever loves the sun, the only way to acquire it is to ascend to it! Everyone understands this is madness.

If one cannot attain his loved object because of a legal barrier that the religion erected, he should convince himself that acquiring his passion is not feasible because Allah did not allow it to happen. He should know that for his safety he should abandon such a matter and be certain that it is virtually impossible that he will ever attain it.

If one's whose heart orders him to do evil does not comply with these cures, let the slave abandon the matter for fear of losing whoever is more beloved to him, more beneficial and who offers a longer period of contentment and joy. In this case, the afflicted person will come to realize the difference between the two objects. Therefore, one should not prefer the joy of an hour that would soon turn to pain comparedtto-eternal joy that is incomparable. The reality of the short-lived joy is that it is a daydream or a mirage that will soon end and fade away, but the responsibility would remain.

In addition, one should realize that this might lead to a hateful loss that is worse for him. than losing the coveted object, and thus his loss would multiply, losing the coveted object and acquiring a hateful outcome. When one realizes these facts, then losing the coveted object: becomes less serious in his heart and. being patient. becomes more worthwhile. The mind, religion, honor and’ human nature then require the afflicted person to be patient in the face of losing a lesser loved object, for in this case, patience would soon earn him joy, contentmentt and happiness. On the other hand, one's ignorance, transience, injustice and immaturity orders him to prefer the lesser coveted object whatever the cost. Those whom Allah gives immunity will be saved from this end.

When one's heart does not accept this solution and

—— The Prophetic Medicine

dislikes using the remedy that we mentioned, let him think about what evil repercussions satisfying his lusts would bring about and what gains it would cause him to lose. He should realize that satisfying his lust is the major cause of an evil outcome in this life, as it prevents the slave from using his mind that is the controller of his actions and which should be used to lead him to benefit instead.

If one's heart still does not accept the remedy we mentioned, let him remember the shortcomings and unlikable characteristics of the coveted object, so that he might dislike the object, as well. When one thinks about this subject, he will realize that such shortcomings supercede the good qualities that made him like that object. Let him In addition ask the neighbors of his loved one about what he does not know of their attributes and behavior. It is a fact that as much as the good qualities drive loved ones closer, the shortcomings drive them apart. Then, one should weigh the two sides and choose the best and most beloved path to take. He should not be among those who are deceived by the color of an otherwise leprous skin. Let one think beyond the beautiful exterior to the true reality of things. Let one look beyond the beautiful shape to the inner evil of the heart.

If all of these remedies do not work, there wi!l be no resort except seeking Allah Who answers the calls of

those in distress when they invoke Him. Let him throw himself before His Door seeking His help with humbleness, humility and meekness.

Those who are led to this type of success should act honorably and keep their affair a secret, so as not to expose the loved one to the people and cause them harm, or else he will be committing a transgression and an injustice.

The Prophet's guidance on preserving the health with perfume

Good scents and perfumes are nutrition for the soul, and the soul is the dynamo of the rest of the body's powers. Perfume helps the brain, the heart and the internal organs and brings comfort to the heart and the soul. Perfumes are In addition the most suitable and favorable remedy and substance for the soul. In addition, there is a close connection between the good soul and scented perfumes. This is why perfumes were among the dearest substances in this world to the heart of the most pure of all people, the Prophet i&.

Al-Bokhary narrated that the Prophet * used to always accept perfume when presented to him. In addition, Muslim narrated that the Messenger of Allah b said:

"He who is offered Raihan (basil) should not refuse it, because it is easy to wear and has a good scent."

Further, Abu Dawood and An-Nasa'y narrated that the Prophet b said:

"Whoever is offered some perfume should not refuse it because it is light to wear and has a good scent."

Ibn Abi Shaibah In addition narrated that the Messenger of Allah bhad a container that had some perfume and he used to perfume himself with it. The Prophet b In addition said:

"Allah has a right on every Muslim that he takes a bath (at least once) in every seven days and if be has perfume to wear some of it.”

Furthermore, the angels like perfume while the devils dislike it, because the dearest scent to the devils are foul odors, while good souls like good scents. Every soul leans towards and deserves what it likes. Evil men are suitable for evil women and vise versa, while good men are suitable for good women and vise versa. Although this statement applies to men and women in general, it In addition applies to every type of deed, speech, food, drink, clothing and scent.

The Prophet's guidance on preserving the health of

the eyes Tbn Ab‘ass narrated:

"Whenever the Messenger of Man used to wear kohl, he would use it thrice in the right eye - beginning with it and finishing with it - and twice in the left eye."

In addition, Abu Dawood narrated that the Prophet b said:

"Those who use Kohl should use it an odd number of times."

One should use an odd number of times when applying Kohl in both eyes, thrice in the right eye and then twice in the left eye. Or, one should use the Kohl thrice in each eye, as Imam Ahmad has stated.

Kohl preserves the health of the eye, strengthens and clears the sight and removes harmful substances, along with adding a touch of beauty to the eye. When one uses kohl before he goes to sleep, it is beneficial for the eye, especially since the eyes will not be moving and thus the kohl will have its most favorable affect. The ithmid type of kohl is especially effective in this regard.

Tbn Majah narrated in his Sunon that Abdullah bin Umar related from the Prophet b that he said:

——— The Prophetic Medicine

"Use Ithmid (antimony), because it clears the eye and grows the eyelashes." [AI-Hakim}.

In addition, Ibn Majah narrated in his Sunan that Ibn Ab'ass related from the Prophet b that he said:

"The best of your kohl is the Ithmid (antimony), because it dears the sight and causes the eyelashes to grow."
content_id:
——— Pengobatan Nabawi

Dalam hal ini, orang yang dicintai bahkan mungkin merasa tidak suka terhadap pihak lain. Kedua, terkadang orang yang dicintai bisa menjadi penyebab cinta itu hanya datang dari satu pihak, seperti kekurangan dalam perilakunya, bentuk, jalan, tindakan, penampilan, dan sebagainya. Ketiga, karena alasan tertentu yang menghalangi orang yang dicintai untuk berbagi perasaan yang sama dengan pihak lain.

Ketika alasan-alasan ini tidak ada, dan cinta itu sungguh nyata, maka cinta akan dimiliki oleh kedua belah pihak.

Kesombongan, hasad, mencintai berbagai kedudukan, dan permusuhan dari orang-orang kafir merupakan alasan utama mengapa para Rasul tidak lebih dicintai oleh mereka melebihi diri mereka sendiri, keluarga, dan keturunan mereka. Ketika penghalang-penghalang ini dihilangkan dari hati para pengikut para Rasul, kecintaan mereka kepada para Rasul sungguh melebihi kecintaan mereka kepada diri sendiri, istri, anak-anak, dan harta benda mereka.

Mendapatkan objek cinta adalah obatnya dan berbagai obat lain untuk cinta

Permasalahannya adalah karena cinta merupakan salah satu penyakit secara umum, maka ia memiliki satu atau beberapa obat. Jika orang yang jatuh cinta memiliki jalan yang sah untuk menggapai objek cintanya, itu akan menjadi obat baginya. Dalam Sahihain diriwayatkan bahwa Ibnu Mas'ud berkata bahwa Nabi صلى الله عليه وسلم bersabda:

"Wahai para pemuda! Barangsiapa di antara kalian yang mampu menikah, maka hendaklah ia menikah. Dan barangsiapa yang belum mampu, hendaklah ia berpuasa, karena puasa itu adalah penawar baginya."

Nabi صلى الله عليه وسلم telah mengarahkan manusia kepada cara terbaik untuk mendapatkan apa yang mereka cintai, yaitu cara besar dan cara kecil. Beliau memerintahkan para pria untuk mengambil pilihan pertama, yaitu pernikahan, yang merupakan obat sempurna bagi penyakit ini. Maka dari itu,

manusia tidak seharusnya lebih mengutamakan solusi lain daripada solusi yang dianjurkan oleh Nabi صلى الله عليه وسلم.

Ibnu Majah meriwayatkan bahwa Nabi صلى الله عليه وسلم bersabda: "Kami tidak pernah melihat sesuatu yang semisal pernikahan bagi orang-orang yang saling mencintai."

Inilah makna yang Allah isyaratkan ketika Dia

membolehkan dalam pernikahan baik wanita merdeka maupun budak wanita ketika diperlukan. Allah berfirman:

"Allah hendak memberikan keringanan kepadamu; dan manusia diciptakan bersifat lemah." (4:28)

Oleh karena itu, Allah menyebutkan kelemahan manusia dalam ayat ini dan menyatakan bahwa Dia telah memudahkan urusan baginya dengan membolehkannya menikah sebanyak yang ia kehendaki, dua, tiga, atau empat. Dia juga membolehkannya menikahi budak wanita jika ia menghendaki, agar dapat mengobati penyakit ini, yaitu nafsu, untuk menyembuhkan kelemahan pada diri manusia dan sebagai bentuk rahmat

——— Pengobatan Nabawi

[dari-Nya] kepada hamba-hamba-Nya.

Ketika tidak ada jalan yang sah bagi orang yang jatuh cinta untuk menggapai objek cintanya

Jika tidak ada jalan yang sah yang dapat ditempuh oleh orang yang jatuh cinta untuk menggapai objek cintanya, atau karena ketidakmampuannya, atau keduanya, maka nafsu akan menjadi penyakit yang berat dalam hal ini, dan orang yang tertimpa musibah ini perlu menyadari bahwa ia tidak dapat memuaskannya. Sudah menjadi kenyataan bahwa ketika hati merasa putus asa terhadap sesuatu, ia tidak akan lagi berusaha untuk mendapatkannya.

Jika penyakit, yakni nafsu, masih ada bahkan dalam kondisi putus asa ini, maka tabiat orang tersebut telah menyimpang dan ia perlu mencoba solusi lain, yaitu memperhatikan kondisi jiwanya. Orang yang ditimpa oleh nafsu harus meyakinkan dirinya bahwa keterikatan hatinya pada sesuatu yang tidak bisa ia raih adalah suatu kegilaan, dan bahwa keadaan ini serupa dengan orang yang mencintai matahari — satu-satunya cara untuk mendapatkannya adalah dengan mendaki ke sana! Semua orang memahami bahwa ini adalah kegilaan.

Jika seseorang tidak dapat menggapai objek cintanya karena adanya penghalang syar'i yang ditetapkan agama, hendaklah ia meyakinkan dirinya bahwa meraih nafsunya tidak mungkin terwujud karena Allah tidak mengizinkannya terjadi. Ia harus tahu bahwa demi keselamatannya ia harus meninggalkan perkara tersebut dan meyakini bahwa mustahil baginya untuk pernah menggapainya.

Jika seseorang yang hatinya memerintahkan keburukan tidak mematuhi obat-obat ini, hendaklah sang hamba meninggalkan perkara tersebut karena takut kehilangan sesuatu yang lebih ia cintai, lebih bermanfaat, dan yang memberikan kesenangan serta kebahagiaan yang lebih lama. Dalam hal ini, orang yang tertimpa musibah tersebut akan menyadari perbedaan antara dua objek. Oleh karena itu, seseorang tidak seharusnya lebih mengutamakan kesenangan sesaat yang segera berubah menjadi kesakitan dibandingkan kesenangan abadi yang tiada bandingnya. Hakikat kesenangan yang fana itu adalah ia bagaikan mimpi di siang bolong atau fatamorgana yang akan segera berakhir dan lenyap, namun tanggung jawabnya tetap ada.

Selain itu, seseorang harus menyadari bahwa hal ini mungkin berujung pada kerugian yang lebih menyakitkan baginya daripada kehilangan objek yang diinginkan, sehingga kerugiannya pun berlipat ganda: kehilangan objek yang diinginkan sekaligus mendapatkan akibat yang buruk. Ketika seseorang menyadari fakta-fakta ini, kehilangan objek yang diinginkan menjadi tidak terlalu berat di hatinya dan bersabar menjadi lebih bernilai. Akal, agama, kehormatan, dan fitrah manusia kemudian menuntut orang yang tertimpa musibah untuk bersabar dalam menghadapi kehilangan objek yang lebih rendah kecintaannya, karena dalam hal ini kesabaran akan segera mendatangkan kegembiraan, ketenangan, dan kebahagiaan. Di sisi lain, kebodohan, kefanaan, kezaliman, dan ketidakdewasaan seseorang memerintahkannya untuk lebih mengutamakan objek yang lebih rendah itu dengan segala cara. Mereka yang Allah berikan perlindungan akan selamat dari akhir yang demikian.

Ketika hati seseorang tidak menerima solusi ini dan

——— Pengobatan Nabawi

enggan menggunakan obat yang kami sebutkan, hendaklah ia merenungkan akibat buruk apa yang akan ditimbulkan oleh pemuasan hawa nafsunya dan keuntungan apa yang akan hilang karenanya. Ia harus menyadari bahwa memuaskan nafsunya adalah penyebab utama dari hasil yang buruk dalam kehidupan ini, karena hal itu menghalangi sang hamba dari menggunakan akalnya yang merupakan pengendali tindakannya dan yang seharusnya digunakan untuk membimbingnya menuju kemanfaatan.

Jika hati seseorang masih tidak menerima obat yang kami sebutkan, hendaklah ia mengingat kekurangan dan sifat-sifat yang tidak disukai dari objek yang diinginkan, sehingga ia mungkin tidak lagi menyukai objek tersebut. Ketika seseorang merenungkan hal ini, ia akan menyadari bahwa kekurangan-kekurangan tersebut melebihi kualitas-kualitas baik yang membuatnya menyukai objek itu. Hendaklah ia juga bertanya kepada para tetangga orang yang dicintainya tentang apa yang belum ia ketahui mengenai sifat dan perilaku mereka. Sudah menjadi kenyataan bahwa sebagaimana kualitas baik mendekatkan orang-orang yang dicintai, kekurangan pun menjauhkan mereka. Kemudian, seseorang harus menimbang kedua sisi dan memilih jalan yang terbaik dan paling dicintai untuk ditempuh. Ia tidak seharusnya termasuk orang-orang yang tertipu oleh warna kulit yang sebenarnya berpenyakit kusta. Hendaklah seseorang memandang melampaui penampilan luar yang indah menuju kenyataan sejatinya. Hendaklah seseorang memandang melampaui bentuk yang indah menuju kejahatan batin hati.

Jika semua obat ini tidak berhasil, tidak ada pilihan lain kecuali memohon kepada Allah Yang mengabulkan seruan orang-orang yang berada dalam kesulitan ketika mereka berdoa kepada-Nya. Hendaklah ia menghempaskan dirinya di depan Pintu-Nya memohon pertolongan-Nya dengan kerendahan hati, ketundukan, dan kehinaan diri.

Mereka yang diberi taufik untuk menempuh jenis keberhasilan ini hendaklah bersikap mulia dan merahasiakan urusan mereka, agar tidak membeberkan orang yang dicintai kepada khalayak ramai dan menyebabkan mereka dirugikan, karena jika tidak, ia akan melakukan pelanggaran dan kezaliman.

Petunjuk Nabi dalam menjaga kesehatan dengan wewangian

Wewangian dan parfum yang baik adalah nutrisi bagi jiwa, dan jiwa adalah dinamo bagi seluruh kekuatan tubuh lainnya. Parfum membantu otak, jantung, dan organ-organ dalam serta memberikan kenyamanan bagi hati dan jiwa. Parfum juga merupakan obat dan zat yang paling cocok dan disukai oleh jiwa. Selain itu, terdapat hubungan yang erat antara jiwa yang baik dan wewangian yang harum. Inilah mengapa wewangian termasuk di antara zat-zat yang paling dicintai di dunia ini oleh hati orang yang paling suci dari seluruh manusia, yaitu Nabi صلى الله عليه وسلم.

Al-Bukhari meriwayatkan bahwa Nabi صلى الله عليه وسلم selalu menerima wewangian ketika ditawarkan kepadanya. Selain itu, Muslim meriwayatkan bahwa Rasulullah صلى الله عليه وسلم bersabda:

"Barangsiapa yang ditawari raihan (kemangi) maka janganlah ia menolaknya, karena ia ringan untuk dibawa dan memiliki wangi yang harum."

Lebih lanjut, Abu Dawud dan An-Nasa'i meriwayatkan bahwa Nabi صلى الله عليه وسلم bersabda:

"Barangsiapa yang ditawari wewangian maka janganlah ia menolaknya, karena ia ringan untuk dibawa dan memiliki wangi yang harum."

Ibnu Abi Syaibah juga meriwayatkan bahwa Rasulullah صلى الله عليه وسلم memiliki sebuah wadah yang berisi wewangian dan beliau biasa memakai wewangian darinya. Nabi صلى الله عليه وسلم juga bersabda:

"Allah memiliki hak atas setiap Muslim untuk mandi (setidaknya sekali) dalam setiap tujuh hari dan jika ia memiliki wewangian hendaklah ia memakainya."

Selanjutnya, para malaikat menyukai wewangian sementara para setan membencinya, karena aroma yang paling disukai setan adalah bau busuk, sedangkan jiwa-jiwa yang baik menyukai wangi yang harum. Setiap jiwa cenderung kepada dan layak mendapatkan apa yang disukainya. Laki-laki yang buruk cocok untuk wanita yang buruk dan begitu pula sebaliknya, sedangkan laki-laki yang baik cocok untuk wanita yang baik dan begitu pula sebaliknya. Meskipun pernyataan ini berlaku untuk laki-laki dan wanita secara umum, ia juga berlaku untuk setiap jenis perbuatan, ucapan, makanan, minuman, pakaian, dan wewangian.

Petunjuk Nabi tentang menjaga kesehatan

mata. Ibnu Abbas meriwayatkan:

"Setiap kali Rasulullah menggunakan celak, beliau menggunakannya tiga kali pada mata kanan — dimulai dan diakhiri dengannya — dan dua kali pada mata kiri."

Selain itu, Abu Dawood meriwayatkan bahwa Nabi bersabda:

"Barang siapa menggunakan celak, hendaklah ia menggunakannya dalam jumlah ganjil."

Seseorang hendaknya menggunakan celak dalam jumlah ganjil pada kedua mata, tiga kali pada mata kanan kemudian dua kali pada mata kiri. Atau, seseorang dapat menggunakan celak tiga kali pada masing-masing mata, sebagaimana yang dinyatakan oleh Imam Ahmad.

Celak menjaga kesehatan mata, menguatkan dan menjernihkan penglihatan serta menghilangkan zat-zat yang berbahaya, sekaligus menambah keindahan pada mata. Ketika seseorang menggunakan celak sebelum tidur, hal itu bermanfaat bagi mata, terutama karena mata tidak akan bergerak sehingga celak akan memberikan pengaruh yang paling baik. Jenis celak ithmid sangat efektif dalam hal ini.

Ibnu Majah meriwayatkan dalam Sunan-nya bahwa Abdullah bin Umar meriwayatkan dari Nabi bahwa beliau bersabda:

——— Pengobatan Nabawi

"Gunakanlah Ithmid (antimoni), karena ia menjernihkan mata dan menumbuhkan bulu mata." [Al-Hakim].

Selain itu, Ibnu Majah meriwayatkan dalam Sunan-nya bahwa Ibnu Abbas meriwayatkan dari Nabi bahwa beliau bersabda:

"Sebaik-baik celak kalian adalah Ithmid (antimoni), karena ia menjernihkan penglihatan dan menumbuhkan bulu mata."
content_ar:
——— The Prophetic Medicine

In this case, the loved person might even feel dislike for the other party. Second, sometimes the loved person might be the cause for the love to be one-sided, such as a deficiency in his mannerism, shape, path, actions, appearance, and so forth. Third, due to a specific reason that prevents the loved person from sharing the same teelings with the other party.

When these reasons are not present, and the love is thus real, love will be shared by both parties.

Arrogance, envy, loving various positions and enmity by the disbelievers were the main reasons the Messengers were not more beloved by them than their own selves, families and offspring. When these barriers were removed from the hearts of the Messengers’ followers, their love for the Messengers was indeed more than the love they had for their own selves, wives, children and wealth.

Attainment of one's love object is its cure and other cures for love

The subject is that since love is one of the ailments in general, it does have a cure or several cures. If the lover has a legal path to take to attain his loved object, it will be his cure. In the Sahihain it is narrated that Ibn Mas'ud said that النبي ﷺ b said:

"O young people! Whoever amongst you can afford marriage, let him marry. Those who cannot afford it should resort to fasting, for it will be a cure for them.”

النبي ﷺ b has directed the people to the best methods to obtain what they love, a major and a minor method. He ordered the men to resort to the first choice, marriage, which is the perfect cure for this disease. Thus,

people should not prefer other solutions to the solution that النبي ﷺ b recommended.

Tbn Majah narrated that النبي ﷺ b said: "We never saw anything like marriage for lovers."

This is the meaning that الله indicated when He

allowed in marriage both free and slave women when needed: الله said:

"الله wishes to lighten (the burden) for you; and man was created weak (cannot be patient to leave sexual intercourse with woman).” (4:28)

Therefore, الله mentioned man's weakness in this Ayah and stated that He has made matters easy for him by allowing him to marry as many as he wishes, two, three or four. He In addition allowed him slave women in marriage if he wishes, so as to treat this ailment, passion, to cure the weakness in mankind and as ar: act of mercy

——— The Prophetic Medicine

[from Him] to His slaves.

When no legal method exists for the lover to attain his love object

If there is no legal method that the lover can attain his loved object or because of his inability to do so, or both, then passion will become a difficult disease in this case and the afflicted person needs to feel that he cannot satisfy it. It is a fact that when the heart feels hopelessness in something, it will no longer seek to acquire it,

If the disease, passion, still exists even under these hopeless conditions, then the nature of the person would have deviated and the person needs to try another solution, that is, attending to his state of mind. The person afflicted by passiun should convince himself that his heart's affection with what it cannot attain constitutes madness, and that this case is similar to whoever loves the sun, the only way to acquire it is to ascend to it! Everyone understands this is madness.

If one cannot attain his loved object because of a legal barrier that the religion erected, he should convince himself that acquiring his passion is not feasible because الله did not allow it to happen. He should know that for his safety he should abandon such a matter and be certain that it is virtually impossible that he will ever attain it.

If one's whose heart orders him to do evil does not comply with these cures, let the slave abandon the matter for fear of losing whoever is more beloved to him, more beneficial and who offers a longer period of contentment and joy. In this case, the afflicted person will come to realize the difference between the two objects. Therefore, one should not prefer the joy of an hour that would soon turn to pain comparedtto-eternal joy that is incomparable. The reality of the short-lived joy is that it is a daydream or a mirage that will soon end and fade away, but the responsibility would remain.

In addition, one should realize that this might lead to a hateful loss that is worse for him. than losing the coveted object, and thus his loss would multiply, losing the coveted object and acquiring a hateful outcome. When one realizes these facts, then losing the coveted object: becomes less serious in his heart and. being patient. becomes more worthwhile. The mind, religion, honor and’ human nature then require the afflicted person to be patient in the face of losing a lesser loved object, for in this case, patience would soon earn him joy, contentmentt and happiness. On the other hand, one's ignorance, transience, injustice and immaturity orders him to prefer the lesser coveted object whatever the cost. Those whom الله gives immunity will be saved from this end.

When one's heart does not accept this solution and

—— The Prophetic Medicine

dislikes using the remedy that we mentioned, let him think about what evil repercussions satisfying his lusts would bring about and what gains it would cause him to lose. He should realize that satisfying his lust is the major cause of an evil outcome in this life, as it prevents the slave from using his mind that is the controller of his actions and which should be used to lead him to benefit instead.

If one's heart still does not accept the remedy we mentioned, let him remember the shortcomings and unlikable characteristics of the coveted object, so that he might dislike the object, as well. When one thinks about this subject, he will realize that such shortcomings supercede the good qualities that made him like that object. Let him In addition ask the neighbors of his loved one about what he does not know of their attributes and behavior. It is a fact that as much as the good qualities drive loved ones closer, the shortcomings drive them apart. Then, one should weigh the two sides and choose the best and most beloved path to take. He should not be among those who are deceived by the color of an otherwise leprous skin. Let one think beyond the beautiful exterior to the true reality of things. Let one look beyond the beautiful shape to the inner evil of the heart.

If all of these remedies do not work, there wi!l be no resort except seeking الله Who answers the calls of

those in distress when they invoke Him. Let him throw himself before His Door seeking His help with humbleness, humility and meekness.

Those who are led to this type of success should act honorably and keep their affair a secret, so as not to expose the loved one to the people and cause them harm, or else he will be committing a transgression and an injustice.

النبي ﷺ's guidance on preserving the health with perfume

Good scents and perfumes are nutrition for the soul, and the soul is the dynamo of the rest of the body's powers. Perfume helps the brain, the heart and the internal organs and brings comfort to the heart and the soul. Perfumes are In addition the most suitable and favorable remedy and substance for the soul. In addition, there is a close connection between the good soul and scented perfumes. This is why perfumes were among the dearest substances in this world to the heart of the most pure of all people, النبي ﷺ i&.

Al-Bokhary narrated that النبي ﷺ * used to always accept perfume when presented to him. In addition, Muslim narrated that رسول الله ﷺ b said:

"He who is offered Raihan (basil) should not refuse it, because it is easy to wear and has a good scent."

Further, Abu Dawood and An-Nasa'y narrated that النبي ﷺ b said:

"Whoever is offered some perfume should not refuse it because it is light to wear and has a good scent."

Ibn Abi Shaibah In addition narrated that رسول الله ﷺ bhad a container that had some perfume and he used to perfume himself with it. النبي ﷺ b In addition said:

"الله has a right on every Muslim that he takes a bath (at least once) in every seven days and if be has perfume to wear some of it.”

Furthermore, the angels like perfume while the devils dislike it, because the dearest scent to the devils are foul odors, while good souls like good scents. Every soul leans towards and deserves what it likes. Evil men are suitable for evil women and vise versa, while good men are suitable for good women and vise versa. Although this statement applies to men and women in general, it In addition applies to every type of deed, speech, food, drink, clothing and scent.

النبي ﷺ's guidance on preserving the health of

the eyes Tbn Ab‘ass narrated:

"Whenever the Messenger of Man used to wear kohl, he would use it thrice in the right eye - beginning with it and finishing with it - and twice in the left eye."

In addition, Abu Dawood narrated that النبي ﷺ b said:

"Those who use Kohl should use it an odd number of times."

One should use an odd number of times when applying Kohl in both eyes, thrice in the right eye and then twice in the left eye. Or, one should use the Kohl thrice in each eye, as Imam Ahmad has stated.

Kohl preserves the health of the eye, strengthens and clears the sight and removes harmful substances, along with adding a touch of beauty to the eye. When one uses kohl before he goes to sleep, it is beneficial for the eye, especially since the eyes will not be moving and thus the kohl will have its most favorable affect. The ithmid type of kohl is especially effective in this regard.

Tbn Majah narrated in his Sunon that Abdullah bin Umar related from النبي ﷺ b that he said:

——— The Prophetic Medicine

"Use Ithmid (antimony), because it clears the eye and grows the eyelashes." [AI-Hakim}.

In addition, Ibn Majah narrated in his Sunan that Ibn Ab'ass related from النبي ﷺ b that he said:

"The best of your kohl is the Ithmid (antimony), because it dears the sight and causes the eyelashes to grow."
